behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: dispatcher must fan out one document plus role-specific instruction packs to three reviewer invocations. A unit test of payload-builder logic can miss wiring bugs (wrong reviewer gets wrong pack). No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and it does not depend on model-output quality. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about dispatch correctness ('sends ... correct ... instruction pack'), not whether reviewer judgments are good.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process fakes for the three reviewer clients; assert each fake receives the same document bytes/id and the correct role pack (extractor/risk/compliance), with no cross-assignment.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model extraction quality ('live clause extractor identifies key terms'). A deterministic stub would always pass and hide failures in interpretation/recall. Single model decision boundary, not multi-agent pipeline correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real clause-extractor model on curated contracts with labeled key terms; score required recall/precision for parties, dates, obligations, and penalties, failing when terms are missed or mislabeled.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about invocation payload wiring, not policy reasoning quality. The real failure appears at boundary between config source and risk-assessor client call. In-process doubles can detect malformed/missing policy fields. Rejected Agentic because text is specifically 'configuration is passed correctly', which disqualifies model-output correctness as the test target.
behaviors.test_description: Wire config loader + invocation service + risk client fake; execute an assessment request and assert the outgoing risk-assessor payload contains the exact configured policy fields/versions and expected defaults.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets semantic judgment quality on subtle phrasing ('live ... flags ... including indirect or euphemistic phrasing'). Stubs cannot validate this; they replace the judgment being tested. Not workflow because it is one reviewer’s model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real risk-assessor model on a benchmark set containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses; assert detection rate against labeled expectations and fail on missed indirect-risk language.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic orchestration/data-mapping at module boundaries (context assembly -> compliance invocation). Real model quality is not the target. Rejected Agentic because the behavior text is only about passing jurisdiction/profile correctly, not whether compliance conclusions are accurate.
behaviors.test_description: Execute compliance-invocation path with in-process fake compliance client; assert outgoing request includes exact jurisdiction code and regulatory profile fields selected for the document/tenant.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real AI compliance judgment ('live compliance checker correctly determines'). Mocked responses hide false positives/negatives. Single agentic decision scope; no required multi-step agent chain in this behavior alone.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance checker against contracts with expert-labeled pass/fail outcomes per requirement profile; assert decision accuracy and critical-requirement miss rate thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Failure is merge-module interaction over structured reviewer outputs; deterministic conflict-resolution logic can be validated with fixed inputs. No real OS/network needed; no dependence on live model quality because conflicting payloads can be injected directly. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is specifically about merge preservation/attribution, not reviewer decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merge service two synthetic reviewer outputs for the same clause with conflicting labels; assert unified report keeps both labels and reviewer attribution, with no overwrite/drop.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans multiple real agentic steps (three live reviewers) plus orchestration and merge. Correctness depends on sequential/parallel handoff across agentic stages and preservation of each stage’s findings. Lower levels miss cross-step loss modes. Rejected pure Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires full pipeline orchestration ('dispatch ... collect ... merge ... without losing any reviewer findings'), i.e., multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline on real contracts using live extractor, risk, and compliance reviewers in parallel; after merge, verify each reviewer’s findings are present (or explicitly attributed conflicts) and none are dropped during collection/merge.
